schema: verdi.decisionconflict/v1
covers: b7641b37f0a81d5212edbab3c99e5b132498b2b0
findings:
  - { id: judged-cf-1, kind: judged, text: "story dc-1 invents a new computation for the size-smell badge — an estimated-rendered-height derivation (AC zone top offset + declared AC count × row pitch, compared to a new reference-viewport-height constant of 900) — while parent dc-1 pins the v1 badge set to 'existing computes only; no computation is invented for a badge'. Size-smell appears in the parent's badge list as an assumed-existing compute, but this story is defining its compute from scratch, and no supersedes/exempts edge against spec/wall-receipts dc-1 is declared. (confidence 0.60)", disposition: no-conflict, note: "wall-receipts dc-1 itself enumerates size-smell as a member of the v1 badge set and ac-5 mandates the badge, so dc-1's no-invention clause closes the set AGAINST computes beyond its own enumeration — it cannot be read to forbid a member of that enumeration, which would make the parent self-contradictory; what this story's dc-1 defines is not a new analysis but ac-5's required determinism resolution (the parent's 'exceeding the viewport' is not computable from pinned inputs without a declared proxy), reading only inputs that already exist (the frontmatter's AC count, the layout package's declared geometry) plus one declared constant — the stub assignment of ac-5 to this story is precisely the parent delegating this definition", target_ref: spec/wall-receipts }
  - { id: judged-cf-2, kind: judged, text: "story dc-4 removes disclosed-unproven pending-supersession from the stamp surface entirely (rendering it as a case-file disclosure line in the board's notice vocabulary), while parent dc-1 places pending-supersession in the v1 wall badge set without any proven/unproven carve-out; the story narrows the parent's badge contract without a declared supersedes/exempts edge. (confidence 0.30)", disposition: no-conflict, note: "unproven is not a value of the flag — it is the absence of proof either way, and pending-supersession stays in the badge set exactly as the parent placed it: the stamp renders whenever the flag is PROVEN flagged; when open MRs cannot be enumerated the computation has no outcome to badge, and dressing that as a stamp would assert a verdict nobody computed (three-valued honesty) while silence would hide the skipped input; the disclosure line is the dex story-lens's own posture for the same state (its unproven metadata row, no badge), and ac-4/co-3 require this surface to be computed identically to that lens — so the rendering follows the parent's same-code-path AC rather than narrowing its badge contract", target_ref: spec/wall-receipts }

Size-smell appears in the parent's badge list as an assumed-existing compute, but this story is defining its compute from scratch, and no supersedes/exempts edge against spec/wall-receipts dc-1 is declared.\",\"confidence\":0.6,\"target\":\"spec/wall-receipts\"},{\"id\":\"cf-2\",\"text\":\"story dc-4 removes disclosed-unproven pending-supersession from the stamp surface entirely (rendering it as a case-file disclosure line in the board's notice vocabulary), while parent dc-1 places pending-supersession in the v1 wall badge set without any proven/unproven carve-out; the story narrows the parent's badge contract without a declared supersedes/exempts edge.\",\"confidence\":0.3,\"target\":\"spec/wall-receipts\"}]}" }

# Decision-conflict report

## Computed (declared-edge completeness)

(none)

## Judged (undeclared-conflict sweep)

- **judged-cf-1** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: story dc-1 invents a new computation for the size-smell badge — an estimated-rendered-height derivation (AC zone top offset + declared AC count × row pitch, compared to a new reference-viewport-height constant of 900) — while parent dc-1 pins the v1 badge set to 'existing computes only; no computation is invented for a badge'. Size-smell appears in the parent's badge list as an assumed-existing compute, but this story is defining its compute from scratch, and no supersedes/exempts edge against spec/wall-receipts dc-1 is declared. (confidence 0.60)
- **judged-cf-2** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: story dc-4 removes disclosed-unproven pending-supersession from the stamp surface entirely (rendering it as a case-file disclosure line in the board's notice vocabulary), while parent dc-1 places pending-supersession in the v1 wall badge set without any proven/unproven carve-out; the story narrows the parent's badge contract without a declared supersedes/exempts edge. (confidence 0.30)
